commented_code:
STACK_VERSION=8.11.3
 
 # The hostname where the Elasticsearch service is exposed
-ES_HOST=es01
+ES_HOST=http://es01:9200

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -31,7 +31,7 @@ COMPOSE_PROFILES=${DOC_ENGINE},${DEVICE}
 STACK_VERSION=8.11.3
 
 # The hostname where the Elasticsearch service is exposed
-ES_HOST=es01
+ES_HOST=http://es01:9200
discussion_comments.comment_body:
Appreciations!
This default setting should not be changed in consideration other users.

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -31,7 +31,7 @@ COMPOSE_PROFILES=${DOC_ENGINE},${DEVICE}
 STACK_VERSION=8.11.3
 
 # The hostname where the Elasticsearch service is exposed
-ES_HOST=es01
+ES_HOST=http://es01:9200
discussion_comments.comment_body:
Hi @KevinHuSh 

There was no actual change in default port or protocol. It was http/9200 before too.

The difference is:

Previously the default protocol (http) and default port (9200) was populated/enforced in the docker/service_conf.yaml.template.
There was no way to define httpS or another port via environment variables.

ES_HOST as environment variable is not used directly but only the after parsing in service_conf.yaml.template

This means for the cases:
No ES_HOST defined: In service_conf.yaml.template the old default is defined: http://es01:9200

ES_HOST defined as old default: http://es01:9200 -> passed through

ES_HOST set to another external service -> passed through

Does this help to clarify?

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -31,7 +31,7 @@ COMPOSE_PROFILES=${DOC_ENGINE},${DEVICE}
 STACK_VERSION=8.11.3
 
 # The hostname where the Elasticsearch service is exposed
-ES_HOST=es01
+ES_HOST=http://es01:9200
discussion_comments.comment_body: The circumstance might be more complicated. By my understanding, this requires `.env` and `service_conf.yaml` should be with the same version since they're coupled. For some users, they might apply the latest .env with the older version of `service_conf.yaml`. So, we should try our best to make minimum alteration to avoid unecessary confusion for all users.

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -31,7 +31,7 @@ COMPOSE_PROFILES=${DOC_ENGINE},${DEVICE}
 STACK_VERSION=8.11.3
 
 # The hostname where the Elasticsearch service is exposed
-ES_HOST=es01
+ES_HOST=http://es01:9200
discussion_comments.comment_body: @KevinHuSh I updated the approach to keep the old ES_HOST format.
This file can not be removed
Oh I see, I'll leave it. It was keep remove my setup, and I didn't check it is removed at PR. Potentially, it would be nice .env to .env.example or something else later.
